data.title: Minco Capital announces grant of stock options
data.description:
• Minco Capital (TSXV:MMM:CA) has approved the grant of an aggregate of 2.7M incentive stock options to directors, officers, and consultants of the company, pursuant to its stock option plan.
  • The options will expire five years from the date of grant and are subject to vesting over a period of 18 months in accordance with the company's stock option plan.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NeuroPace secures $75M credit facility with MidCap Financial
data.description:
• NeuroPace (NASDAQ:NPCE) entered into a new $75M credit facility with MidCap Financial, comprising a $60M term loan and $15M revolving credit facility.
  • Proceeds from the term loan were used to fully repay an existing loan with CRG Partners IV, L.P.
  • The revolving credit facility will support working capital and corporate initiatives.
  • The financing provides non-dilutive capital at improved terms, reducing cash interest expense.
  • The new facility has 5-year maturity.
  • NPCE shares rise over 4% post-market on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: BRT Apartments declares $0.25 dividend
data.description:
• BRT Apartments (NYSE:BRT) declared $0.25/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 6.22%
  • Payable July 9; for shareholders of record June 25; ex-div June 25.
  • See BRT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Lulu’s Fashion Lounge CFO to step down
data.description:
• Lulu’s Fashion Lounge (NASDAQ:LVLU) announced that Tiffany R. Smith, chief financial officer, is stepping down from her role effective June 30, 2025.
  • Upon Smith’s departure, Crystal Landsem, chief executive officer, will assume the responsibilities of interim chief financial officer, alongside her responsibilities as chief executive officer. 
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Cipher Mining produces 179 BTC in May, prepares for hashrate expansion
data.description:
• Cipher Mining (NASDAQ:CIFR) produced 179 bitcoin (BTC) in May 2025, including ~4 BTC from power sales and ~23 BTC from joint venture sites.
  • CIFR sold 64 BTC during the month as part of regular treasury operations.
  • BTC holdings stood at 966 BTC at month-end, including ~334 BTC pledged as collateral.
  • 75,000 mining rigs were deployed, with an operating hashrate of 13.5 EH/s and fleet efficiency of 18.9 J/TH. The
  • Black Pearl Phase I site is nearly complete, with legacy rigs from Odessa already racked and awaiting energization. Cipher has purchased enough mining rigs to fill 150 MW of capacity at Black Pearl Phase I.
  • New rigs are expected to arrive in early July, and full deployment is projected to raise Cipher’s total hashrate to ~23.1 EH/s.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Greif Non-GAAP EPS of $1.19, revenue of $1.39B
data.description:
• Greif press release (NYSE:GEF): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.19.
  • Revenue of $1.39B (+1.5% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBITDA(2) increased 26.0% to $213.9 million compared to Adjusted EBITDA of $169.7 million.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities increased by $48.9 million to a source of $136.4 million. Adjusted free cash flow(3) increased by $50.6 million to a source of $109.6 million.
  • Total debt of $2,775.2 million decreased by $140.9 million. Net debt(4) decreased by $197.6 million to $2,522.5 million. Our leverage ratio(5) decreased to 3.3x from 3.4x in the prior year quarter.
  • Fiscal 2025 Low-EndGuidance EstimateReported at Q2
    Adjusted EBITDA $725
    Adjusted free cash flow $280
  • Shares +2.42%.

data.title: Main Street Financial announces CEO transition
data.description:
• Main Street Financial Services (OTCQX:MSWV) terminated chief executive officer and president Jay R. VanSickle II, effective June 3, 2025.
  • VanSickle is no longer a member of the board.
  • Mark R. Witmer, executive chair and director since 2024, has been appointed president and CEO.
  • Witmer will retain his role as chairman of the Board.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: ChargePoint Holdings GAAP EPS of -$0.12 beats by $0.01, revenue of $97.64M misses by $2.94M
data.description:
• ChargePoint Holdings press release (NYSE:CHPT): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.12 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $97.64M (-8.8% Y/Y) misses by $2.94M.
  • First quarter fiscal 2026 GAAP gross margin of 29% and non-GAAP gross margin of 31%
  • First quarter fiscal 2026 subscription revenue of $38 million representing 14% year-over-year growth
  • First quarter fiscal 2026 GAAP operating expense of $82 million and non-GAAP operating expense of $57 million, representing 10% and 15% year-over-year reduction
  • Q2 Outlook: For the second fiscal quarter ending July 31, 2025, ChargePoint expects revenue of $90 million to $100 million vs. consensus of $108.35M.
  • ChargePoint remains committed to its plans of achieving positive non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA during a quarter in fiscal year 2026.
  • Shares -11.6%.

data.title: CyberArk announces $750M convertible note offering due 2030
data.description:
• CyberArk Software (NASDAQ:CYBR) plans to offer $750M of 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2030 in a private offering to qualified institutional buyers.
  • An additional $125M of notes may be sold if the initial purchasers exercise their 13-day option.
  • The notes are senior unsecured obligations, maturing June 15, 2030, and convertible into cash, shares, or both, subject to conditions.
  • Proceeds will be used for capped call costs, working capital, and possibly future acquisitions or investments. Any unused proceeds will be invested in short-term, high-quality fixed income instruments.
  • CYBR shares fall 2.9% post-market on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release
